title: Implementation Card
description: Backend-specific implementation of an Op Spec. Many cards point at one Op Spec. Validated against that Op Spec's one reference + tolerances. See docs/library.md §2.2.

implements.description: The Op Spec id this card satisfies.

backend.description: Maps 1:1 to xkernels._backends.Backend for callable resolution.

family.description: Architecture family. 'any' for backend-neutral (e.g. reference, or portable Triton). nvidia_sm100 = Blackwell data-center (B100/B200); nvidia_sm121 = Grace+Blackwell GB10 (CUTE DSL test bed).

wave_size.description: 32 (NVIDIA warp) | 64 (AMD wavefront) | 0 (agnostic). Never hardcode 32 in a kernel above this card.

specialization_knobs.description: Per-backend tuning space. Map of knob name -> {type, choices} (or {type:'int', min, max} for ranges). Empty for reference.

regime.description: Free-form per-arch regime hint, e.g. 'memory-bound tall-skinny'.

measured.description: Write-back measurements. Each MUST cite a reproducible `source` run id and an `arch`; un-sourced or arch-less entries are dropped by the loader.

uses_primitives.description: Primitive ids this card is assembled from. Enables bug-traceability across cards/backends.

supersedes.description: Impl card ids this one replaces (lineage).

authored_by.description: Who authored the card. 'dsl' = generated by the vkl DSL emitter (docs/brainstorm/); contract-identical to a hand-written card.

derived_from.description: Impl card id this was ported/derived from.

issue.description: GitHub issue number(s) this card resolves or refs (AGENTS.md: record the issue number in any new/changed card's provenance.issue).

source_path.description: Repo-relative path to the source the card describes.

tuning_trace.description: Namespaced vkl compounding artifact (docs/brainstorm/09 §6). Append-only log of edit steps; the next run reads it to skip dead-ends. Each entry: {step, edit, target, args, check, predicted, measured}. Ignored by the bottom (JSON-only) consumption tier.

launch.description: Namespaced vkl launch/orchestration metadata (docs/brainstorm/07 §7). The bottom tier ignores it; a graph-aware runtime uses launch.graph to capture. additionalProperties left open so future launch modes stream-add without a schema bump.

graph.description: True if this card is a captured CUDA/HIP graph rather than a single launch.

nodes.description: Ordered kernel node ids in the graph.

persistent.description: Optional namespaced block for persistent-grid / on-chip dataflow orchestration metadata for fused or persistent-grid (mega)kernels. Peer of `launch` (which covers CUDA/HIP-graph capture); the bottom JSON tier ignores it. The sub-fields below are NOW PINNED (meta/docs/design/megakernel-blockers.md (b) point 1) so a megakernel card can declare its on-chip dataflow contract honestly and an agent can reason about applicability from metadata — but additionalProperties stays open so new orchestration modes stream-add without a schema bump (the structure is still in flux). Vocabulary mirrors the vkl schedule IR (Stage(id, producer_ref, space, depth) in vkl/schedule.py) and cost model (predict_scratch / register-half occupancy in vkl/cost.py).

residency.description: The intermediate tensor names this kernel keeps ON-CHIP (registers/LDS) for the whole persistent grid — i.e. they never round-trip to DRAM. The defining property of a megakernel vs a graph of separate launches; empty means a single-op kernel (no cross-op staging).

warp_roles.description: Named roles warps play in the persistent grid (e.g. 'producer', 'consumer', 'epilogue', 'reducer'). Lets an agent reason about the partitioning without reading the kernel source.

register_budget.description: The register half of the occupancy model (vkl/cost.py keeps this profile-calibrated). regs/thread and whether the budget forces spills.

spills.description: True if the budget forces register spills to scratch.

lds_budget.description: The on-chip scratch footprint (LDS on AMD, shared memory on NVIDIA), in bytes, for the persistent grid's staged tiles (vkl/cost.py predict_scratch). Peer of arch.scratch but for the whole staged pipeline.

stages.description: Pipeline depth the staged tiles are buffered at (num_stages).

pipeline_stages.description: The staged tiles (vkl schedule IR: Stage(id, producer_ref, space, depth)). Each entry is one buffer a producer warp fills and a consumer warp drains.

depth.description: Pipeline depth — a concrete int, or a knob name resolved at emit. Untyped on purpose (int OR string).

defused_edges.description: Producer->consumer edges in the dataflow that are deliberately NOT fused (the intermediate IS written to DRAM and read back), named 'producer->consumer'. The complement of `residency`: a megakernel minimizes this set; empty `defused_edges` with non-empty `residency` is a fully on-chip persistent kernel.

notes.description: Free-form agent/human notes (e.g. portability caveats: wave-size 32 vs 64 is not load-bearing here).
